creator: knu@idaemons.org
text:
At Sat, 5 May 2001 22:40:07 -0700 (PDT),
Yoichi NAKAYAMA wrote:
>  I see. Thanks a lot.
>  by the way, portlint cannot check the following
>  
>  > - The RCS tag you put for the first import is $FreeBSD$, not $FreeBSD: $.
>  
>  and, there is another trap ;-) when there is no "$FreeBSD: $" line :
>  
>      FATAL: no $FreeBSD$ line in Makefile comment section.
>  
>  Isn't it a bug of portlint?

Try portlint -N when you are going to check a new port.

>  > By the way, what about making dcc a separate port that irchat-pj-* and
>  > liece-* depend on?  All those four ports install ${LOCALBASE}/bin/dcc
>  > and it will be mercilessly removed when any of them is deinstalled...
>  
>  I think the separation of emacs-lisp and binaries is a good idea.
>  I'll try, but there is one question.
>  What the VERSION of dcc, ldcc ports should be?
>  Are there some rule for fixing such version numbers?

Maybe just the RCS revision number of the main (dcc.c or something)
source file will do in most cases.
